title: TeraWulf Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
text: TeraWulf reported full year 2025 revenue of $168.5 million, a 20% increase from $140.1 million in 2024. Digital assets contributed $151.6 million and HPC contributed $16.9 million in full year 2025. Q4 2025 revenue totaled $35.8 million, with digital asset revenue of $26.1 million (down from $43.4 million in Q3) due to lower bitcoin production and pricing, and HPC lease revenue of $9.7 million (up from $7.2 million in Q3). Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA was negative $23.1 million for full year 2025. The company's cash position reached $3.7 billion. TeraWulf achieved significant infrastructure progress with 522 critical IT MW under long-term data center leases worth over $12.8 billion in potential revenue. The company secured $6.5 billion in committed financing to support expansion. Lake Mariner has 39 MW operational with contracts totaling 440 MW and multiple buildings scheduled for completion through Q4 2026. The Abernathy Joint Venture in Texas has 168 MW capacity with Google credit enhancement, with construction progressing toward 2026 delivery.
